analysis.note: Correlative with tāvat; final -t is voiced to -d before v- in external sandhi.

samasa.vigraha: vijñaptir eva mātram; tasya bhāvaḥ vijñaptimātratvam

analysis.note: Abstract -tva derivative from vijñaptimātra; the locative gives the sphere/standpoint: “in representation-only-ness.”

verification.detail: compound stem vijñaptimātratva as Napumsaka/Saptami/Eka: pass; relation karmadharaya: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: The neuter nominative and accusative singular forms coincide; here it is construed as nominative subject of the intransitive avatiṣṭhate.
External sandhi: na + avatiṣṭhate → nāvatiṣṭhate; the hyphen marks a shared sandhi vowel.
Ātmanepada 3rd singular present form; the schema has no separate pada field. The initial a- is coalesced with preceding na in the printed nāvatiṣṭhate.
Genitive singular depending on anuśayaḥ; final -a coalesces with the initial a- of anuśayaḥ in the printed sandhi.

verification.detail: compound stem grāhadvaya as Napumsaka/Sasthi/Eka: pass; relation tatpurusa: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Subject of vinivartate. The initial a- is included in the preceding shared ā; final visarga appears as -s before tāvat.
Correlative with yāvat; final -t assimilates to -n before na.
Ātmanepada 3rd singular present, used intransitively: “turns back, ceases, disappears.” The schema has no separate pada field.

justifications.decision: The two clauses are linked by the standard yāvat/tāvat correlation, not by two independent quantitative adjectives.
options_considered: correlative adverbial construction: “as long as ... so long”
separate quantitative uses: “as much as ... so much”
justifications.chosen: yāvat ... tāvat as an adverbial correlative: “so long as ... so long.”

evidence.translation: The paired indeclinables frame the two finite clauses as “so long as ... so long.”

justifications.decision: The ending -tve is read as the locative singular of an abstract -tva stem built on vijñapti-mātra; mātra is not an independent measured quantity here.
options_considered: literal measure reading: “in the measure of a report/request”
analysis of tva as an independent lexical item rather than abstract suffix
justifications.chosen: vijñaptimātratve = locative singular of vijñaptimātratva, “in representation-only-ness.”

evidence.translation: The form is locative singular in -e of the neuter abstract stem vijñaptimātratva, “the state of being only vijñapti.”

justifications.decision: Although vijñānam could formally be nominative or accusative, the intransitive finite verb makes it the subject. The bhāṣya reading supplied here has -te, so the analysis follows the middle form rather than the vulgate active -ti.
options_considered: vijñānam as nominative subject of avatiṣṭhate
following the variant active avatiṣṭhati instead of the supplied -te reading
justifications.chosen: vijñānam is the nominative subject; avatiṣṭhate is middle/intransitive “takes its stand, abides.”

evidence.translation: “Consciousness does not take its stand”; the negated middle present is intransitive.

justifications.decision: The genitive compound depends on anuśayaḥ. Lexically grāha is taken in its “seizing/grasping” sense, and dvaya supplies the dyadic/twofold value.
options_considered: tatpuruṣa: grāhayoḥ dvayam, “the dyad of grasping”
a non-philosophical reading of grāha as a water-animal/crocodile
an adjective-only reading, “two grasping things,” without genitive dependence on anuśayaḥ
justifications.chosen: grāhadvayasya is genitive singular of grāhadvaya, “of the twofold grasping / dyad of grasping.”

evidence.translation: “The latent tendency of the twofold grasping”: -sya marks the genitive relation to anuśayaḥ.

justifications.decision: The nominative singular anuśayaḥ agrees syntactically with the 3rd singular middle verb vinivartate; the second na negates the cessation/reversal of that latent tendency.
options_considered: causative sense: “does not cause to turn back”
justifications.chosen: anuśayaḥ is the subject of vinivartate; vinivartate means “ceases / turns back” intransitively.

evidence.translation: “So long the latent tendency does not cease / turn back.”

translation:
So long as consciousness (vijñāna) does not take its stand
in representation-only-ness (vijñaptimātratva),
so long the latent tendency (anuśaya)
of the twofold grasping (grāhadvaya) does not cease.
one_shot_delta: A commentary-blind or dictionary-only rendering could miss the yāvat/tāvat frame and translate the two clauses as unrelated quantities rather than “so long as ... so long.”
It could take vijñapti and mātra in ordinary dictionary senses (“report/request” and “measure”) instead of the technical compound “representation-only.”
It could overread grāhadvaya as a fully specified pair such as grāhya/grāhaka; without commentary in this run, the safer rendering is only “twofold grasping.”
It could follow the vulgate -ti form and translate an active avatiṣṭhati, whereas the supplied bhāṣya reading is the middle avatiṣṭhate.
analyzer_disagreements: For avatiṣṭhate, ByT5 reports the lexicalized form avasthā; per the requested format I give the bare verbal root sthā with prefix ava.
For vinivartate, ByT5 reports the prefixed lemma vinivṛt; per the requested format I give the bare verbal root vṛt with prefixes vi and ni.
For vijñaptimātratve, ByT5’s segmentation vijñapti-mātra-tve is retained semantically, but the inflected nominal is represented as the single abstract stem vijñaptimātratva, locative singular neuter.
ByT5 does not disambiguate yāvat/tāvat as finite-clause correlatives; I mark both as avyaya on grammatical grounds.
open_questions: No commentary span was supplied for this ablation run, so the exact Yogācāra unpacking of grāhadvaya cannot be verified from bhāṣya evidence here.
Without commentary, the precise doctrinal nuance of avatiṣṭhate—simple “abiding,” “standing firm,” or a specifically contemplative stabilization—remains underdetermined.
